what_it_does: Defines the dragnet stage-1 coarse extraction role as a registry-style ROLE dictionary, binding a frozen neutral prompt template and an imported output schema for neutral chunk description.
observations: The file contains a single module-level ROLE dictionary with id, label, description, prompt_template, and output_schema.
It imports Coarse and COARSE_PROMPT from contracts.dragnet_schema and contains no functions or runtime logic.
The docstring states this role was previously constructed inline and is now a file-discovered registry row.
It is described as a NEUTRAL stage-1 pass producing the coarse superset facet {about, kind, touches} with no relevance or topic judgement.
It is marked as PROTECTED / suite-owned, so edit_role and delete_role operations refuse changes.
The prompt template and schema are described as frozen and exact-match enforced by an external _build_role door.
standouts: Prompt template and output schema are externally frozen and not authored in this file.
The role is suite-owned protected, implying registry tooling refuses edits/deletes.
It explicitly forbids relevance/topic judgement in the coarse pass.
conventions: Uses an imported uppercase constant COARSE_PROMPT for the canonical frozen prompt.
Uses an imported schema class Coarse as the output_schema value.
Exposes a module-level ROLE dict for registry discovery.
Docstring records provenance, protection status, and design rationale.
concerns: Correctness depends entirely on external enforcement and schema definitions not visible here.
No runtime validation, tests, or error handling are present in the file.
The description string is built with manual concatenation, which is slightly brittle.
notes: This is a thin, protected registry entry that wires a frozen neutral prompt and schema into the dragnet coarse stage.
questions: What exact checks does the _build_role freeze door perform, and where is it implemented?
How is suite-owned protection enforced at runtime?
What are the full fields of the Coarse schema beyond {about, kind, touches}?
How does the registry system discover and load this file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of providing a neutral stage-1 coarse extraction role as a registry row matches the actual content, which is a ROLE dictionary binding a frozen prompt and schema.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make the dragnet coarse extractor a first-class, immutable, registry-discoverable component whose prompt and schema cannot be silently forked or biased.
novelty: It elevates an inline programmatic role construction into a protected file-discovered registry row, with explicit external enforcement of prompt and schema immutability.
contribution: Provides a stable, neutral, stage-1 coarse extraction role entry that a fused system can reference by id, with a bound frozen prompt and schema, protected from ad-hoc mutation.
summary_for_embedding: A protected registry role file that defines the dragnet stage-1 neutral coarse extraction role by binding a frozen prompt template and imported Coarse schema.
intention_for_embedding: To expose the dragnet coarse extractor as a configurable yet immutable registry component with enforced neutrality and frozen contracts.
